content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
note: My feature: fix resource leak in shell completion by wrapping make_context calls with 'with' statements in _resolve_context (shell_completion.py) and possibly minor core.py context changes. Need to know your feature/files to avoid overlap.

fields.note: My feature: fix resource leak in shell completion by wrapping make_context calls with 'with' statements in _resolve_context (shell_completion.py) and possibly minor core.py context changes. Need to know your feature/files to avoid overlap.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Both our features touch _resolve_context in shell_completion.py at the same lines (the make_context call sites), so full file overlap there is unavoidable if we both edit it. Proposal: I own shell_completion.py entirely (wrapping the 3 make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements to fix the resource leak) and you own core.py entirely (validate_nesting param on Context). If your feature also needs a change inside shell_completion.py's make_context calls (e.g. passing validate_nesting=False), tell me the exact kwarg/line you need and I'll fold it into my rewrite of _resolve_context so we don't get conflicting edits on the same lines. Otherwise this split is fully disjoint.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll wrap all 3 make_context call sites in _resolve_context with 'with' statements and add validate_nesting=False to each of them (cli.make_context, cmd.make_context for sub_ctx, and cmd.make_context for sub_sub_ctx). Starting implementation now.
